887	/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)
888	
889	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
890	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
891	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
892	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
893	`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
894	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
895	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
896	domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
897	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
898	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/
899	
900	section NagataNormalization
901	
902	-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
903	-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
904	open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List
905	
906	/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
907	private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
908	    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
909	    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
910	      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
911	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
912	  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
913	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
914	  | mul_X p i hp =>
915	    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
916	    congr 1
917	    cases i using Fin.cases with
918	    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
919	    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]
920	
921	variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
922	variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)
923	
924	local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
925	local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1
926	
927	omit [IsDomain k] in
928	variable {f v} in
929	private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind
930	
931	/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
932	`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
933	private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
934	    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
935	  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i
936	
937	omit [IsDomain k] in
938	private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
939	  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp
940	
941	/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
942	private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
943	  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))
944	
945	omit [IsDomain k] in
946	private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
947	    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
948	  intro h
949	  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
950	  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
951	    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
952	  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
953	    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h
954	
955	private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
956	    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
957	  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
958	  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
959	    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,
960	    ite_false, one_smul, map_add, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, finSuccEquiv_X_succ, algebraMap_eq]
961	  have h (i : Fin n) :
962	      (Polynomial.C (X (R := k) i) + Polynomial.X ^ r i.succ) ^ v i.succ ≠ 0 :=
963	    pow_ne_zero (v i.succ) (leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp <| by simp [add_comm, leadingCoeff_X_pow_add_C])
964	  rw [natDegree_mul (by simp [ha]) (mul_ne_zero (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
965	    (fun i _ ↦ h i))), natDegree_mul (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun i _ ↦ h i)),
966	    natDegree_prod _ _ (fun i _ ↦ h i), natDegree_finSuccEquiv, degreeOf_C]
967	  simpa only [natDegree_pow, zero_add, natDegree_X, mul_one, Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_mul,
968	    add_right_inj] using Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ ↦ by
969	    rw [add_comm (Polynomial.C _), natDegree_X_pow_add_C, mul_comm])
970	
971	private lemma degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne (hv : v ∈ f.support) (hw : w ∈ f.support) (ne : v ≠ w) :
972	    (T f <| monomial v <| coeff v f).degreeOf 0 ≠
973	    (T f <| monomial w <| coeff w f).degreeOf 0 := by
974	  rw [degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hv, degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hw]
975	  refine sum_r_mul_ne f v w (fun i ↦ ?_) (fun i ↦ ?_) ne <;>
976	  exact lt_of_le_of_lt ((monomial_le_degreeOf i ‹_›).trans (degreeOf_le_totalDegree f i)) (by lia)
977	
978	private lemma leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t :
979	    (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) ((monomial v) (coeff v f)))).leadingCoeff =
980	    algebraMap k _ (coeff v f) := by
981	  rw [monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_fintype]
982	  · simp only [map_mul, map_prod, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_prod]
983	    rw [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, algHom_C, algebraMap_eq, finSuccEquiv_apply, eval₂Hom_C, coe_comp]
984	    simp only [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, Function.comp_apply, leadingCoeff_C, map_pow,
985	      leadingCoeff_pow, algebraMap_eq]
986	    have : ∀ j, ((finSuccEquiv k n) ((T1 f) 1 (X j))).leadingCoeff = 1 := fun j ↦ by
987	      by_cases h : j = 0
988	      · simp [h, finSuccEquiv_apply]
989	      · simp only [aeval_eq_bind₁, bind₁_X_right, if_neg h, one_smul, map_add, map_pow]
990	        obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := Fin.exists_succ_eq.mpr h
991	        simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, add_comm]
992	    simp only [this, one_pow, Finset.prod_const_one, mul_one]
993	  exact fun i ↦ pow_zero _
994	
995	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
996	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
997	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
998	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
999	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1000	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1001	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1002	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1003	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1004	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1005	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1006	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1007	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1008	    intro x hx
1009	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1010	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1011	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1012	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2
1013	  have coeff : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v + ∑ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, h x))).leadingCoeff =
1014	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1015	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1016	    rw [add_comm]
1017	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1018	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1019	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1020	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq
1021	    exact (Finset.sup_lt_iff <| Ne.bot_lt (fun x ↦ h2 <| degree_eq_bot.mp x)).mpr vs
1022	  refine ⟨v, vin, ?_⟩
1023	  rw [leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t] at coeff
1024	  nth_rw 2 [← f.support_sum_monomial_coeff]
1025	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton_of_mem vin h, coeff, algebraMap_eq]
1026	
1027	/-- **L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables: monic in the distinguished variable**
1028	(`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a domain and `0 ≠ F ∈
1029	A[X_0,…,X_m]`, there is a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a non-zero
1030	`g ∈ A` such that, after inverting `g`, the image of `e F` in `A_g[X_0,…,X_m]` —
1031	viewed via `finSuccEquiv` as a univariate polynomial in `X_0` — has a *unit* leading
1032	coefficient. (Variable convention: `finSuccEquiv` singles out `X_0`, not `X_m`; the
1033	choice is immaterial up to renaming.) -/
1034	theorem gf_nagata_monic_lastVar
1035	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (m : ℕ)
1036	    (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
1037	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1038	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),
1039	      IsUnit
1040	        (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) m
1041	          (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff := by
1042	  obtain ⟨v, vin, hlc⟩ := T_leadingcoeff_eq F hF
1043	  have hg0 : coeff v F ≠ 0 := mem_support_iff.mp vin
1044	  refine ⟨coeff v F, hg0, T F, ?_⟩
1045	  set g := coeff v F with hg
1046	  haveI : Nontrivial (Localization.Away g) := by
1047	    have hle : Submonoid.powers g ≤ nonZeroDivisors A := by
1048	      rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0
1049	    exact (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away g) hle).nontrivial
1050	  have hgunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g) :=
1051	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g
1052	  have hCunit : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g)
1053	      : MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) := hgunit.map MvPolynomial.C
1054	  have hne : (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)))
1055	      (finSuccEquiv A m (T F F)).leadingCoeff ≠ 0 := by
1056	    rw [hlc, MvPolynomial.map_C]; exact hCunit.ne_zero
1057	  rw [finSuccEquiv_map_comm, leadingCoeff_map_of_leadingCoeff_ne_zero _ hne, hlc,
1058	    MvPolynomial.map_C]
1059	  exact hCunit
1060	
1061	end NagataNormalization
1062	
1063	/-- Compatibility of `finSuccEquiv` with the constant-variable inclusion
1064	`rename Fin.succ`: the composite `S = R[X_1,…,X_n] → R[X_0,…,X_n] → S[X_0]` is the
1065	coefficient inclusion `Polynomial.C`. Project-local because Mathlib only records the
1066	`optionEquivLeft` variant (`finSuccEquiv_rename_finSuccEquiv`). -/
1067	private theorem finSuccEquiv_rename_succ
1068	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :
1069	    MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s) = Polynomial.C s := by
1070	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1071	  | C r => rw [MvPolynomial.rename_C, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]; rfl
1072	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1073	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1074	    rw [map_mul, map_mul, hp, MvPolynomial.rename_X, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_X_succ,
1075	      Polynomial.C_mul]
1076	
1077	/-- **L5b.3 — single-variable elimination engine** (shared)
1078	(`lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic`, Nitsure §4). Let `R` be a commutative
1079	ring and `p ∈ R[X_0,…,X_n]` a polynomial whose image under `finSuccEquiv` (a
1080	univariate polynomial in `X_0` over `S := R[X_1,…,X_n]`) has a *unit* leading
1081	coefficient — i.e. `p` is monic in `X_0` up to a unit. Then `R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)` is
1082	module-finite over `S = R[X_1,…,X_n]`, the structure being via the constant
1083	inclusion `S ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n]` (`rename Fin.succ`) followed by `Ideal.Quotient.mk`.
1084	
1085	Encoded as `RingHom.Finite` of that composite ring map (which unfolds to
1086	`Module.Finite S (R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p))` with the exotic algebra structure) to avoid an
1087	in-statement `letI` whose `Module` synthesis loops. The proof rescales `finSuccEquiv p`
1088	by the inverse of its unit leading coefficient to a monic polynomial generating the
1089	same ideal, invokes `Polynomial.Monic.finite_quotient`, and transports along the
1090	`S`-algebra isomorphism induced by `finSuccEquiv`. -/
1091	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
1092	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
1093	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
1094	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
1095	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1096	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
1097	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
1098	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
1099	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1100	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
1101	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
1102	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
1103	  have hcP :
1104	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
1105	    hc.map Polynomial.C
1106	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1107	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
1108	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
1109	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
1110	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
1111	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1112	      = Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p} :=
1113	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)
1114	  have hfinstd : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1115	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) := by
1116	    rw [← hspan]; exact hmonic.finite_quotient
1117	  have hJ : Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1118	      = Ideal.map ((MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv :
1119	          MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R →+* Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R))
1120	        (Ideal.span {p}) := by
1121	    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
1122	  let ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1123	      ≃+* (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
1124	    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {p}) (Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1125	      (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv hJ
1126	  have hsmul : ∀ s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R,
1127	      ψ (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1128	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) s)
1129	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1130	          (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1131	          s := by
1132	    intro s
1133	    change ψ (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s))
1134	      = Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C s)
1135	    rw [Ideal.quotientEquiv_mk]
1136	    exact congrArg _ (finSuccEquiv_rename_succ R n s)
1137	  let Ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1138	      ≃ₐ[MvPolynomial (Fin n) R]
1139	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
1140	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv hsmul
1141	  exact @Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1142	    (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1143	    (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1144	    _ _ _ _ algI.toModule hfinstd Ψ.symm.toLinearEquiv
1145	
1146	/-- Pull back an `R`-module structure along an additive equivalence `e : M ≃+ N`, defining
1147	`r • y := e (r • e.symm y)`. With this structure `e` is `R`-linear; used in the torsion
1148	reindex to transport the reindexed module structure from the `P`-localisation of the torsion
1149	module to the goal's `A`-localisation. -/
1150	@[reducible] def pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1151	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] (e : M ≃+ N) : Module R N where
1152	  smul r y := e (r • e.symm y)
1153	  one_smul y := by change e (1 • e.symm y) = y; rw [one_smul, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1154	  mul_smul r s y := by
1155	    change e ((r * s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm (e (s • e.symm y)))
1156	    rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, mul_smul]
1157	  smul_zero r := by change e (r • e.symm 0) = 0; rw [map_zero, smul_zero, map_zero]
1158	  smul_add r x y := by
1159	    change e (r • e.symm (x + y)) = e (r • e.symm x) + e (r • e.symm y)
1160	    rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
1161	  add_smul r s y := by
1162	    change e ((r + s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm y) + e (s • e.symm y)
1163	    rw [add_smul, map_add]
1164	  zero_smul y := by change e (0 • e.symm y) = 0; rw [zero_smul, map_zero]
1165	
1166	/-- Module-finiteness transports across the pulled-back structure of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1167	if `M` is a finite `R`-module then so is `N` under the pulled-back action. -/
1168	theorem finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1169	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] (e : M ≃+ N) :
1170	    @Module.Finite R N _ _ (pullbackModuleAddEquiv e) := by
1171	  letI : Module R N := pullbackModuleAddEquiv e
1172	  refine Module.Finite.equiv (M := M) (N := N)
1173	    { toFun := e, map_add' := map_add e, invFun := e.symm,
1174	      left_inv := e.left_inv, right_inv := e.right_inv,
1175	      map_smul' := fun r x => ?_ }
1176	  simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1177	  change e (r • x) = e (r • e.symm (e x))
1178	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
1179	
1180	/-- A scalar tower transports across the pulled-back structures of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1181	if `e : M ≃+ N` and `M` carries compatible `Ag`- and `R`-actions (`IsScalarTower Ag R M`), then
1182	the pulled-back `Ag`- and `R`-actions on `N` form a scalar tower as well. -/
1183	theorem pullback_isScalarTower {Ag R M N : Type*} [CommSemiring Ag] [Semiring R] [Algebra Ag R]
1184	    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module Ag M] [IsScalarTower Ag R M]
1185	    (e : M ≃+ N) :
1186	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1187	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1188	    IsScalarTower Ag R N := by
1189	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1190	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1191	  refine ⟨fun a b x => ?_⟩
1192	  change e ((a • b) • e.symm x) = e (a • e.symm (e (b • e.symm x)))
1193	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, smul_assoc]
1194	
1195	/-- Transport module-finiteness across a ring isomorphism of the acting ring that is
1196	compatible with given `R`-algebra structures. If `ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂` satisfies
1197	`ψ ∘ algebraMap R B₁ = algebraMap R B₂`, `B₂` is module-finite over `R`, and `M` is
1198	module-finite over `B₁` (with the scalar tower `R → B₁ → M`), then `M` is module-finite

1200	theorem finite_of_quotientRingEquiv
1201	    {R B₁ B₂ M : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂] [AddCommGroup M]
1202	    [Algebra R B₁] [Algebra R B₂] [Module B₁ M] [Module R M] [IsScalarTower R B₁ M]
1203	    (ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂)
1204	    (hψ : ∀ r : R, ψ (algebraMap R B₁ r) = algebraMap R B₂ r)
1205	    (hB₂ : Module.Finite R B₂) (hM : Module.Finite B₁ M) :
1206	    Module.Finite R M := by
1207	  haveI := hB₂
1208	  haveI := hM
1209	  have hsymm : ∀ r : R, ψ.symm (algebraMap R B₂ r) = algebraMap R B₁ r := by
1210	    intro r; rw [← hψ r, ψ.symm_apply_apply]
1211	  haveI : Module.Finite R B₁ :=
1212	    Module.Finite.equiv (AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := ψ.symm) hsymm).toLinearEquiv
1213	  exact Module.Finite.trans B₁ M

1215	/-- Descent of a localized-module structure along a scalar tower. If `f : M →ₗ[R] M'`
1216	localises `M` at the image submonoid `S.map (algebraMap R' R)` over `R`, then its
1217	restriction of scalars to `R'` localises `M` at `S` over `R'`. -/
1218	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars
1219	    {R' R M M' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [CommRing R] [Algebra R' R]
1220	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M] [Module R M] [Module R' M'] [Module R M']
1221	    [IsScalarTower R' R M] [IsScalarTower R' R M']
1222	    (S : Submonoid R') (f : M →ₗ[R] M')
1223	    [IsLocalizedModule (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f] :
1224	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R') := by
1225	  apply IsLocalizedModule.mk
1226	  · intro s
1227	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1228	    have hmem : algebraMap R' R (s : R') ∈ S.map (algebraMap R' R) := ⟨s, s.2, rfl⟩
1229	    have hu := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨algebraMap R' R (s : R'), hmem⟩
1230	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hu
1231	    have hfun : ⇑((algebraMap R' (Module.End R' M')) (s : R'))
1232	        = ⇑((algebraMap R (Module.End R M')) (algebraMap R' R (s : R'))) := by
1233	      funext x
1234	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
1235	    rw [hfun]
1236	    exact hu
1237	  · intro y
1238	    obtain ⟨⟨t, sm⟩, hsm⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f y
1239	    obtain ⟨s, hsS, hs⟩ := sm.2
1240	    refine ⟨⟨t, ⟨s, hsS⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1241	    change (s : R') • y = (f.restrictScalars R') t
1242	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') y, hs]
1243	    exact hsm
1244	  · intro x₁ x₂ h
1245	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply] at h
1246	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S.map (algebraMap R' R)) (f := f) h

1248	    refine ⟨⟨s, hsS⟩, ?_⟩
1249	    change (s : R') • x₁ = (s : R') • x₂
1250	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₁,
1251	        ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₂, hs]
1252	    exact hc

1493	-- The doubly-localised carrier `LocalizedModule (powers h) (LocalizedModule (powers g) T)`
1494	-- makes instance search for the transported `Localization.Away (g*a)`-action explore many
1495	-- `OreLocalization`/`LocalizedModule` paths; raise the synthesis budget accordingly.
1496	/-- **Descent of generic freeness across a tower of `Away` localisations.** If after
1497	inverting `g ∈ A` and then `h ∈ A_g = Localization.Away g` the `A`-module `T` becomes
1498	free, then it becomes free after inverting a single non-zero `f ∈ A`.
1499	
1500	This is the "descend the witness from `A_g` to `A`" step (step 4) of the polynomial-ring
1501	core `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`: the inductive hypothesis is applied at the
1502	new base `A_g` and produces freeness of the *iterated* localisation
1503	`(T_g)_h := LocalizedModule (powers h) (LocalizedModule (powers g) T)` over `(A_g)_h`. Here
1504	`h = a / gᵏ`, so `(A_g)_h ≅ A_{g·a}` (`IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated`) and the
1505	iterated localisation is the single localisation of `T` at `powers (g·a)`; transporting
1506	freeness across the ring iso (`Module.Free.of_ringEquiv`) and the localised-module
1507	identification gives `T_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·a`. -/
1508	theorem free_localizationAway_of_away_tower
1509	    (A T : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [AddCommGroup T] [Module A T]
1510	    {g : A} (hg : g ≠ 0) {h : Localization.Away g} (hh : h ≠ 0)
1511	    (hfree : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
1512	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))) :
1513	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1514	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) T) := by
1515	  classical
1516	  -- Witness `f := g · a`, where `a ∈ A` is a numerator of `h` over `A_g`.
1517	  -- === Ring side: clear the denominator of `h` ===
1518	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers g) h
1519	  -- `hs : h * algebraMap A A_g ↑s = algebraMap A A_g a`, with `s ∈ powers g` a unit denominator.
1520	  have hsunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A)) :=
1521	    IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away g) s
1522	  -- `algebraMap ↑s` is the unit `u`; record `algebraMap ↑s * u⁻¹ = 1`.
1523	  have hsu : algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹) = 1 := by
1524	    have h1 := hsunit.unit.mul_inv
1525	    rwa [hsunit.unit_spec] at h1
1526	  -- `a ≠ 0`: else `h * algebraMap ↑s = 0`, and `algebraMap ↑s` is a unit, forcing `h = 0`.
1527	  have ha : a ≠ 0 := by
1528	    rintro rfl
1529	    rw [map_zero] at hs
1530	    apply hh
1531	    calc h = h * (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹)) := by
1532	            rw [hsu, mul_one]
1533	      _ = h * algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹) := by rw [mul_assoc]
1534	      _ = 0 := by rw [hs, zero_mul]

1536	  -- `algebraMap a` and `h` are associated (differ by the unit `algebraMap ↑s`).
1537	  have hassoc : Associated (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a) h := by
1538	    refine ⟨hsunit.unit⁻¹, ?_⟩
1539	    rw [← hs, mul_assoc, hsu, mul_one]
1540	  -- The canonical tower `A → A_g → A_h` already holds; `A_h` is then the localisation of `A`
1541	  -- away from `g · a` (`mul_of_associated`).

1543	    (IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated g a h hassoc :
1544	      IsLocalization.Away (g * a) (Localization.Away h))
1545	  -- === Module side: the composite localisation map and its `IsLocalizedModule` structure ===
1546	  -- `ψ : T →ₗ[A] D` factoring `T → T_g → D` (restrict the second map to `A`).

1548	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1549	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)
1550	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)).restrictScalars A ∘ₗ
1551	      LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers g) T
1552	  -- The `A`-action on `D` factors through `A_h`.

1554	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) := by
1555	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun a' x => ?_
1556	    rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away h) a',
1557	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization.Away h),
1558	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization.Away g)]
1559	  -- Localisation-of-localisation as a base change: `D` is the base change of `T` to `A_h`.
1560	  have hbcψ : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away h) ψ :=
1561	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers g) (Localization.Away g)
1562	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers g) T)).comp
1563	      (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers h) (Localization.Away h)
1564	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)
1565	          (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)))
1566	  haveI hLMψ : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) ψ :=
1567	    (isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) (Localization.Away h) ψ).mpr hbcψ
1568	  -- === Transport freeness from `A_h` down to `A_{g·a}` ===
1569	  refine ⟨g * a, hf0, ?_⟩
1570	  -- `A_{g·a} ≃ₐ[A] A_h` (two localisations of `A` away from `g · a`).
1571	  let σ : Localization.Away (g * a) ≃ₐ[A] Localization.Away h :=
1572	    IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) (Localization.Away (g * a))
1573	      (Localization.Away h)
1574	  -- The `A`-linear uniqueness iso between the two localisations of `T` at `powers (g·a)`.
1575	  let ε : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T ≃ₗ[A]
1576	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1577	    IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers (g * a))
1578	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T) ψ
1579	  -- Equip `D` with the `A_{g·a}`-action transported through `σ` (acting via `σ`).
1580	  letI modLga : Module (Localization.Away (g * a))
1581	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) :=
1582	    Module.compHom
1583	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
1584	      (σ.toAlgHom.toRingHom : Localization.Away (g * a) →+* Localization.Away h)
1585	  -- The `A`-action through this new `A_{g·a}`-action is the original one.
1586	  haveI towerLga : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away (g * a))
1587	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) := by
1588	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun a' x => ?_
1589	    change σ (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (g * a)) a') • x = a' • x
1590	    rw [AlgEquiv.commutes]
1591	    exact IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization.Away h) a' x
1592	  -- `D` is free over `A_{g·a}`: transport a basis of `D` over `A_h` through the ring iso `σ.symm`.
1593	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
1594	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) := hfree
1595	  have hcompat : ∀ (c : Localization.Away h)
1596	      (x : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
1597	      (σ.symm.toRingEquiv c) • x = c • x := fun c x => by
1598	    change σ (σ.symm c) • x = c • x
1599	    rw [AlgEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1600	  have hDfree : Module.Free (Localization.Away (g * a))
1601	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) :=
1602	    Module.Free.of_basis
1603	      ((Module.Free.chooseBasis (Localization.Away h)
1604	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h)
1605	          (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))).mapCoeffs σ.symm.toRingEquiv hcompat)
1606	  -- The `A`-linear `ε` upgrades to `A_{g·a}`-linear; conclude freeness of `T_{g·a}`.
1607	  have εL : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (g * a)]
1608	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1609	    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (g * a))
1610	      (Localization.Away (g * a)) ε
1611	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv' hDfree εL.symm

1613	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
1614	-- The reindexed localised quotient `(N ⧸ range φ)_g` carries stacked
1615	-- `OreLocalization`/`MvPolynomial` module structures whose instance search is expensive;
1616	-- raise the synthesis budget for the inductive-step wiring.
1617	/-- **L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness** (`lem:gf_polynomial_core`,
1618	Nitsure §4, the genuine Mathlib-absent residue). For `A` a noetherian domain and
1619	`d ≥ 0`, a finite module `N` over the polynomial ring `A[X_1,…,X_d]`, regarded as
1620	an `A`-module via the scalar tower `A → A[X] → N`, becomes free after inverting a
1621	single non-zero `f ∈ A`.
1622	
1623	This is the bottom of the Nitsure §4 induction. The base case `d = 0` is the
1624	finite-`A`-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`
1625	(`MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`). The inductive step builds the generic-rank short
1626	exact sequence `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0` with `T` torsion of support
1627	dimension `< d`, applies the inductive hypothesis to `T` and the torsion base
1628	case `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion`, and splices via
1629	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact`. -/
1630	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial
1631	    (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1632	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]
1633	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
1634	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
1635	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1636	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
1637	  -- The genuine proof is a *strong* induction on `d`, with the module `N` and its
1638	  -- instances universally quantified in the motive: the inductive hypothesis must
1639	  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
1640	  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
1641	  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The
1642	  -- `generalizing N` reverts `N` together with all five of its `d`-dependent
1643	  -- instances, so the IH has the shape
1644	  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
1645	  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
1646	  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
1647	  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
1648	  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
1649	  | _ d IH =>
1650	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
1651	    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
1652	      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
1653	      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
1654	      subst hd
1655	      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
1656	        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
1657	      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
1658	      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
1659	    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
1660	      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
1661	      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
1662	        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
1663	        -- ring of definition.
1664	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors
1665	      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,
1666	        -- let `m` be the generic rank of `N` over the domain `A[X_1,…,X_d]`; choosing
1667	        -- `m` elements whose images form a `K(X)`-basis and clearing denominators
1668	        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
1669	        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
1670	        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
1671	        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
1672	        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
1673	        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
1674	        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
1675	        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
1676	        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
1677	        --
1678	        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
1679	        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
1680	        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
1681	        --
1682	        -- The generic-rank SES `0 → (Fin m → P) → N → T → 0` (`gf_generic_rank_ses`),
1683	        -- with `T := N ⧸ range φ` torsion over `P := A[X_1,…,X_d]`.
1684	        obtain ⟨m, φ, hφinj, hTtors⟩ := gf_generic_rank_ses A d N
1685	        -- Step 1: the torsion cokernel `N ⧸ range φ` is a finite `P`-module (its restricted
1686	        -- `A`-module structure + scalar tower `A → P → quotient` are found by instances).
1687	        haveI hTfin : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) :=
1688	          Module.Finite.of_surjective (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1689	        -- Step 2: reindex the torsion cokernel onto `m' < d` variables over `A_g`.
1690	        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` now emits its `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-action (`hmod1`) and the
1691	        -- scalar tower (`htower`) over the *canonical* (`inferInstance`) `Module A_g`-action on the
1692	        -- localised module — the redundant `Module A_g T_g` existential was dropped, so the
1693	        -- `A_g`-action the IH and `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` synthesise is exactly the
1694	        -- one `htower` refers to.  This dissolves the former `OreLocalization`
1695	        -- instance-presentation diamond: there is now a single `Module A_g T_g` instance in play.
1696	        obtain ⟨g, hg0, m', hm'lt, hmod1, htower, hfin⟩ :=
1697	          gf_torsion_reindex A d hd (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hTtors
1698	        -- Step 3: the inductive hypothesis at the new noetherian-domain base `A_g`.
1699	        haveI hdomg : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
1700	          IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
1701	            (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0))
1702	        haveI hnoethg : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) := inferInstance
1703	        -- Register the reindex's `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-module structure, its finiteness, and
1704	        -- its scalar tower in the local instance cache so the IH application infers them.
1705	        letI := hmod1
1706	        haveI := hfin
1707	        haveI := htower
1708	        -- Steps 3–4: the IH at base `A_g` produces `hh ≠ 0` in `A_g` with the doubly-localised
1709	        -- `(T_g)_hh` free over `(A_g)_hh`; the tower-descent helper
1710	        -- `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` descends that witness to a single `f := g·a ∈ A`.
1711	        obtain ⟨f, hf0, hTf_free⟩ :
1712	            ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1713	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)) := by
1714	          obtain ⟨hh, hh0, hfree_Tgh⟩ :=
1715	            IH m' hm'lt (Localization.Away g)
1716	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ))
1717	          exact free_localizationAway_of_away_tower A (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hg0 hh0 hfree_Tgh

1721	        haveI hM'free_base : Module.Free A (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) := inferInstance
1722	        have hM'_free : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1723	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)) :=
1724	          (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
1725	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f)
1726	              (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A))).free
1727	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
1728	          (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) φ
1729	          (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ hφinj (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1730	          (by rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ]) hf0 hf0 hM'_free hTf_free

1732	end GenericFreeness

1734	/-! ## Generic flatness, algebraic form (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness")
1735	
1736	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
1737	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1738	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.
1739	
1740	The proof splits along the blueprint's decomposition:
Let me continue reading the Lean file to see the `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` and `genericFlatness` declarations.

1741	
1742	* **Primary route** (provided here, axiom-clean): when `M` is already
1743	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1744	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the
1745	  localised module is a finite vector space hence free, and
1746	  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` descends that
1747	  freeness to a single basic open `D(f)`.
1748	
1749	* **Surviving residue** (`sorry` this iter): when `M` is finite over the
1750	  *finite-type* algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A`, the genuine §4
1751	  dévissage is required — a prime filtration of `M` as a finite `B`-module
1752	  reduces to `M = B/𝔭`, Noether normalisation makes `B_g` finite over the
1753	  polynomial ring `A_g[b₁,…,b_n]`, and induction on the support dimension
1754	  bottoms out at the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness. That core
1755	  (a finite module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]` is generically free) is the precise
1756	  piece Mathlib does not yet supply. -/
1757	
1758	/-- **Generic flatness, algebraic form** (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic
1759	Flatness"). Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and
1760	`M` a finite `B`-module regarded as an `A`-module via the scalar tower
1761	`A → B → M`. Then there exists `f ∈ A`, `f ≠ 0`, such that
1762	`LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M` is free over `Localization.Away f`.
1763	
1764	Blueprint: `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`. The finite-`A`-module case is the
1765	thin Mathlib wrapper `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`;
1766	the finite-type residue is the classical §4 dévissage (still `sorry`). -/
1767	theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic
1768	    (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1769	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
1770	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
1771	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
1772	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1773	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
1774	  by_cases hAM : Module.Finite A M
1775	  · -- Primary route: `M` module-finite over `A` ⟹ the finite-module helper.
1776	    haveI := hAM
1777	    exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
1778	  · -- Surviving residue: `M` finite over the finite-type algebra `B` but not

1	\chapter{Flattening Stratification of a Coherent Sheaf}
2	\label{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4
6	% \emph{generic flatness} theorem: over a noetherian integral base, a coherent
7	% sheaf on a finite-type \(X \to S\) becomes flat over a non-empty open
8	% subscheme of \(S\). Both its algebraic form (generic freeness of a finite
9	% module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian domain) and its
10	% geometric form are recorded here as the prover-ready specification for
11	% \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}.
12	%
13	% This chapter is independently parallel-startable: it does not gate on the
14	% Route~A.1.a relative-Spec chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}.
15	
16	\section{Setup and motivation}
17	\label{sec:flatstrat_setup}
18	
19	Generic flatness is the foundational input to the flattening-stratification
20	construction underlying Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Quot scheme
21	(\cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}, \S4--\S5).  Its content is that flatness of a
22	coherent sheaf is a generic property over an integral base: over a noetherian
23	integral scheme \(S\), a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type morphism
24	\(X \to S\) becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open subscheme of
25	\(S\). Algebraically this is the statement that a finite module over a
26	finite-type algebra over a noetherian domain becomes free after inverting a
27	single non-zero element of the base ring.
28	
29	We work throughout with a noetherian (integral, where required) base scheme
30	\(S\), a finite-type morphism \(p : X \to S\), and a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
31	\(\F\).
32	
33	\section{Generic flatness}
34	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
35	
36	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
37	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
38	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
39	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
40	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
41	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
42	non-zero element of the base ring.
43	
44	\begin{theorem}
45	
46	
47	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
48	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
49	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}
50	  \uses{lem:gf_torsion_base, lem:noeth_prime_filtration, lem:gf_splice_shortExact,
51	        lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators, lem:gf_polynomial_core, lem:gf_finite_module}
52	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''
53	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
54	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
55	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
56	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
57	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
58	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
59	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
60	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
61	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
62	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
63	
64	\end{theorem}
65

67	% exactly this signature):

74

78	% of \(M_K\) over \(\spec B_K\). We argue by induction on \(n\), starting
79	% with \(n=-1\) which is the case when \(M_K =0\). [...]

81	% As \(B\) is noetherian and \(M\) is assumed to be a finite \(B\)-module,
82	% there exists a finite filtration

84	% where each \(M_i\) is a \(B\)-submodule of \(M\) such that for each \(i\ge 1\)
85	% the quotient module \(M_i/M_{i-1}\) is isomorphic to \(B/\pp_i\) for
86	% some prime ideal \(\pp_i\) in \(B\). [...] By Noether normalisation lemma,
87	% there exist elements \(b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B\), such that \(K\otimes_AB\) is
88	% finite over its subalgebra \(K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]\) [...]"
89	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1772; full
90	% proof omitted for length, structural steps restated below in project notation.)
91	\begin{proof}
92	
93	  \textbf{Primary route — the finite \(A\)-module case.}
94	  Suppose first that \(M\) is module-finite over \(A\) itself. Since \(A\) is noetherian,
95	  \(M\) is finitely presented over \(A\). The generic fibre \(M_K = K \otimes_A M\)
96	  (where \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\)) is a finite-dimensional \(K\)-vector space, hence free
97	  over \(K\); by \cref{lem:gf_finite_module} this freeness descends to a single basic open:
98	  there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\). This closes the
99	  theorem whenever \(M\) is finite over \(A\).
100	
101	  \textbf{Surviving residue — reducing the finite-type \(B\)-algebra case to the
102	  finite \(A\)-module case.} The genuine content not supplied by Mathlib is the
103	  reduction of the general hypothesis (\(M\) finite over a \emph{finite-type}
104	  \(A\)-algebra \(B\)) to the finite \(A\)-module situation handled above. Concretely,
105	  the missing piece is the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness: a finite module
106	  over \(A[b_1, \dots, b_n]\) becomes free over \(A_f\) after inverting a single
107	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\). The classical \S4 dévissage below is the fallback route
108	  for exactly this residue.
109	
110	  \emph{Fallback (Nitsure \S4 induction).}
111	  The \S4 dévissage is assembled from the sub-lemmas of
112	  \cref{sec:gf_devissage_chain}. If \(M_K = 0\), the torsion base case
113	  \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} gives \(f \ne 0\) with \(M_f = 0\) free. Otherwise the
114	  prime-filtration induction principle \cref{lem:noeth_prime_filtration} over the
115	  noetherian ring \(B\), combined with the splicing fact
116	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact}, reduces to \(M = B/\mathfrak{p}\) and hence to
117	  \(B\) a domain, \(M = B\); Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators
118	  \cref{lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators} makes \(B_g\) finite over a polynomial
119	  ring \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), and the polynomial-ring core
120	  \cref{lem:gf_polynomial_core} — bottoming out at the finite-\(A\)-module leaf
121	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module} of the primary route — produces the required
122	  \(f \ne 0\) with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\).
123	\end{proof}
124	
125	\subsection{Dévissage chain for the finite-type residue}
126	\label{sec:gf_devissage_chain}
127	
128	The following sub-lemmas decompose the surviving residue of
129	\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} — the case where \(M\) is finite over the
130	finite-type \(A\)-algebra \(B\) but not module-finite over \(A\) — into the
131	classical \cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}~\S4 induction. They are ordered so that each block
132	depends only on earlier blocks or Mathlib lemmas. Throughout,
133	\(A\) is a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\), \(B\) a
134	finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module with the compatible
135	\(A\)-module structure via the scalar tower \(A \to B \to M\).
136	
137	\begin{lemma}[Finite-module leaf — finite \(A\)-module case]
138	  \label{lem:gf_finite_module}
139	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite}
140	  \uses{lem:fp_free_descent}
141	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(M\) a finite \(A\)-module. Then there
142	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
143	\end{lemma}
144	
145	\begin{proof}
146	
147	  This is the \(d = 0\) leaf of the induction. A finite module over a noetherian ring is
148	  finitely presented, so
149	  \(M\) is finitely presented over \(A\). Over the generic point
150	  \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A) = S^{-1}A\) (\(S = A \setminus \{0\}\)) the localisation
151	  \(M_K = S^{-1}M\) is a finite-dimensional \(K\)-vector space, hence free over
152	  \(K\). The finite-presentation free-descent lemma \cref{lem:fp_free_descent}
153	  then descends this freeness to a single basic open: there is \(r \in S\), i.e.\
154	  \(r \in A\) with \(r \ne 0\), such that \(M_r\) is free over \(A_r\). Take
155	  \(f := r\).
156	\end{proof}
157	
158	\begin{lemma}[Finite-presentation free descent]
159	  \label{lem:fp_free_descent}
160	  \lean{Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers}
161	  \mathlibok
162	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
163	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(S \subseteq R\) a multiplicative subset, and
164	  \(M\) a finitely-presented \(R\)-module such that the localisation \(M_S\) is
165	  free over the localisation \(R_S\). Then there exists \(r \in S\) such that
166	  \(M_r\) is free over \(R_r\) (of the same finite rank).
167	\end{lemma}
168	
169	\begin{lemma}[L1 — torsion base case \(M_K = 0\)]
170	  \label{lem:gf_torsion_base}
171	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion}
172	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, base case
173	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1726).
174	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
175	  % "We argue by induction on $n$, starting with $n=-1$ which is the case when
176	  %  $M_K =0$. In this case, as $K\otimes _AM = S^{-1}M$ where $S = A - \{ 0\}$,
177	  %  each $v\in M$ is annihilated by some non-zero element of $A$. Taking a finite
178	  %  generating set, and a common multiple of corresponding annihilating elements,
179	  %  we see there exists an $f\ne 0$ in $A$ with $fM=0$. Hence $M_f =0$, proving
180	  %  the lemma when $n=-1$."
181	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (base case of the
182	  induction).}
183	  Suppose \(K \otimes_A M = 0\), where \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\). Then there exists
184	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(f M = 0\); consequently \(M_f = 0\) is free over
185	  \(A_f\).
186	\end{lemma}
187	
188	\begin{proof}
189	
190	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1719-L1726).
191	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (base case).}
192	  Since \(K \otimes_A M = S^{-1}M\) with \(S = A \setminus \{0\}\), the hypothesis
193	  \(M_K = 0\) means every element of \(M\) is killed by some non-zero element of
194	  \(A\). Choose a finite \(B\)-generating set \(v_1, \dots, v_k\) of \(M\) (which
195	  exists because \(M\) is a finite \(B\)-module), and for each \(i\) an
196	  \(a_i \in A\), \(a_i \ne 0\), with \(a_i v_i = 0\). The product
197	  \(f := a_1 \cdots a_k\) is non-zero (\(A\) is a domain) and annihilates every
198	  generator, hence \(f M = 0\). Therefore \(M_f = 0\), which is free over \(A_f\).
199	\end{proof}
200	
201	\begin{lemma}[L2 — prime-filtration induction principle]
202	  \label{lem:noeth_prime_filtration}
203	  \lean{IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime}
204	  \mathlibok
205	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
206	  Let \(B\) be a noetherian commutative ring and let \(P\) be a predicate on finite
207	  \(B\)-modules that (i) holds for every subsingleton module, (ii) holds for every
208	  cyclic quotient \(B/\mathfrak{p}\) with \(\mathfrak{p}\) prime, and (iii) is
209	  closed under short exact sequences (\(P(N_1)\) and \(P(N_3)\) imply \(P(N_2)\)
210	  for \(0 \to N_1 \to N_2 \to N_3 \to 0\)). Then \(P\) holds for every finite
211	  \(B\)-module. In particular every finite \(B\)-module \(M\) admits a finite
212	  filtration \(0 = M_0 \subset \cdots \subset M_r = M\) with each quotient
213	  \(M_i / M_{i-1} \cong B/\mathfrak{p}_i\) for a prime \(\mathfrak{p}_i\), so to
214	  prove generic freeness for \(M\) it suffices to prove it for each
215	  \(B/\mathfrak{p}\) and to splice along the filtration's short exact sequences.
216	\end{lemma}
217	
218	\begin{lemma}[L3a — localisation of a short exact sequence is exact]
219	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact}
221	  Let \(0 \to M' \xrightarrow{i} M \xrightarrow{q} M'' \to 0\) be a short exact
222	  sequence of \(B\)-modules, and let \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\). Restricting scalars
223	  along \(A \to B\) and localising every term at the powers of \(f\) yields a short
224	  exact sequence
225	  \[
226	    0 \to M'_f \xrightarrow{i_f} M_f \xrightarrow{q_f} M''_f \to 0
227	  \]
228	  of \(A_f\)-modules; in particular \(i_f\) is injective and \(q_f\) is surjective.
229	\end{lemma}
230	
231	\begin{proof}
232	
233	  Localisation at a multiplicative set is an exact functor, preserving injections,
234	  surjections, and exactness. Applying it to the scalar-restricted \(A\)-linear maps
235	  \(i\) and \(q\) (via \(A \to B\)) returns the displayed sequence. Proved directly in Lean.
236	\end{proof}
237	
238	\begin{lemma}[L3b — free transport across a finer localisation]
239	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport}
240	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul}
241	  Let \(N\) be a \(B\)-module and \(f', f'' \in A\) with \(f = f' f''\). If the
242	  localisation \(N_{f'}\) is free over \(A_{f'}\), then the further localisation
243	  \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
244	\end{lemma}
245	
246	\begin{proof}
247	
248	  Because \(f = f' f''\), the localisation away from \(f\) factors through the
249	  localisation away from \(f'\): the ring \(A_f\) is the localisation of \(A_{f'}\)
250	  at the image of \(f''\), and likewise the module \(N_f\) is the localisation of
251	  \(N_{f'}\) at the image of \(f''\). A localisation of a free module is free over
252	  the localised base ring — a basis of \(N_{f'}\) over \(A_{f'}\) maps to a basis of
253	  \(N_f\) over \(A_f\). Hence \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Stated as a standalone
254	  fact, it is applied to both ends of the spliced sequence.
255	\end{proof}
256	
257	\begin{lemma}[L3c — a short exact sequence with free ends has a free middle]
258	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split}
259	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_of_shortExact_of_free_free}
260	  Let \(0 \to M'_f \to M_f \to M''_f \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of
261	  \(A_f\)-modules in which both ends \(M'_f\) and \(M''_f\) are free over \(A_f\).
262	  Then \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\); concretely \(M_f \cong M'_f \oplus M''_f\).
263	\end{lemma}
264	
265	\begin{proof}
266	
267	  A free module is projective, so the free quotient \(M''_f\) is projective and the
268	  short exact sequence splits: \(M_f \cong M'_f \oplus M''_f\). A finite direct sum
269	  of free \(A_f\)-modules is free, hence \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
270	\end{proof}
271	
272	\begin{lemma}[L3 — splicing fact for a short exact sequence (assembly)]
273	  \label{lem:gf_splice_shortExact}
274	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact}
275	  \uses{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact,
276	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split}
277	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, splicing remark
278	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1737-L1745).
279	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
280	  % "Note that if $0\to M'\to M\to M'' \to 0$ is a short exact sequence of
281	  %  $B$-modules, and if $f'$ and $f''$ are non-zero elements of $A$ such that
282	  %  $M'_{f'}$ and $M''_{f''}$ are free over respectively $A_{f'}$ and $A_{f''}$,
283	  %  then $M_f$ is a free module over $A_f$ where $f=f'f''$. We will use this fact
284	  %  repeatedly. Therefore it is enough to prove the result when $M$ is of the form
285	  %  $B/\pp$ for a prime ideal $\pp$ in $B$. This reduces us to the case where $B$
286	  %  is a domain and $M=B$."
287	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (splicing fact).}
288	  Let \(0 \to M' \to M \to M'' \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of \(B\)-modules,
289	  and let \(f', f'' \in A\) be non-zero with \(M'_{f'}\) free over \(A_{f'}\) and
290	  \(M''_{f''}\) free over \(A_{f''}\). Then for \(f := f' f''\) the localisation
291	  \(M_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
292	\end{lemma}
293	
294	\begin{proof}
295	  \uses{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact,
296	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split}
297	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1737-L1745).
298	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (splicing fact).}
299	  Set \(f := f' f''\); since \(A\) is a domain and \(f', f'' \ne 0\), the product is
300	  non-zero, so \(f \ne 0\). Localising the given short exact sequence at the powers
301	  of \(f\) (after restricting scalars to \(A\)) produces a short exact sequence
302	  \(0 \to M'_f \to M_f \to M''_f \to 0\) of \(A_f\)-modules, by
303	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact}. Both ends are free over \(A_f\):
304	  applying \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport} to \(M'\) with the
305	  factorisation \(f = f' f''\) (using that \(M'_{f'}\) is free over \(A_{f'}\)) shows
306	  \(M'_f\) is free over \(A_f\), and applying the same lemma to \(M''\) with the
307	  factorisation \(f = f'' f'\) (using that \(M''_{f''}\) is free over \(A_{f''}\))
308	  shows \(M''_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Finally
309	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split} concludes that the middle term \(M_f\) is
310	  free over \(A_f\).
311	\end{proof}

313	\begin{lemma}[L4 anchor — Noether normalisation over a field]
314	  \label{lem:noether_normalization_fg}
315	  \lean{exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg}
316	  \mathlibok
317	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
318	  Let \(k\) be a field and \(R\) a non-trivial finite-type \(k\)-algebra. Then there
319	  exist \(s \in \mathbb{N}\) and an injective \(k\)-algebra homomorphism
320	  \(k[X_1, \dots, X_s] \to R\) that is module-finite, i.e.\ \(R\) is a finite module
321	  over a polynomial subalgebra on algebraically independent generators.
322	\end{lemma}
323	
324	\begin{lemma}
325	
326	[L4a — clearing one equation's denominators]
327	  \label{lem:gf_clear_one_denominator}
328	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_clear_one_denominator}
329	  % NOTE (shared engine): the denominator-clearing performed here -- pushing finitely
330	  % many coefficients of one polynomial from K = Frac(A) down into a single
331	  % Localization.Away g -- is the same primitive the torsion reindex (L5b,
332	  % lem:gf_torsion_reindex) uses when it inverts the leading coefficient of the
333	  % Nagata-normalised annihilator. A prover who writes one general "clear finitely many
334	  % denominators into one Localization.Away g" helper can have both lemmas consume it.
335	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, normalisation step
336	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1755-L1759).
337	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
338	  % "If $g\ne 0$ in $A$ is chosen to be a `common denominator' for
339	  %  coefficients of equations of integral dependence
340	  %  satisfied by a finite set of
341	  %  algebra generators for $K\otimes_AB$ over $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$,
342	  %  we see that $B_g$ is finite over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
343	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
344	  %   Let K := FractionRing A and A_g := Localization.Away g; the canonical map
345	  %   A_g -> K (induced because powers of g are non-zero-divisors of A) extends along
346	  %   MvPolynomial.map to MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g -> MvPolynomial (Fin n) K.
347	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
348	  %       (p : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (FractionRing A)) :
349	  %       ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0),
350	  %         p ∈ Set.range
351	  %           (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (FractionRing A)))
352	  % NOTE (encoding): there is NO canonical `Algebra (Localization.Away g)
353	  %   (FractionRing A)` instance, so the landed Lean does not use the literal
354	  %   `algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (FractionRing A)` above. It encodes the same map
355	  %   as `IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A) hle` with
356	  %   `hle : powers g ≤ comap id (nonZeroDivisors A)` — mathematically identical, just the
357	  %   well-typed form. The proof is axiom-clean.
358	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (common denominator).}
359	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\), and let
360	  \(p \in K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) be a polynomial with coefficients in \(K\) (for instance,
361	  one coefficient polynomial of an equation of integral dependence over
362	  \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\)). Then there exists \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), such that \(p\)
363	  lies in the image of the coefficient-extension map
364	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\); equivalently, \(p\) has all of its
365	  coefficients in \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\).
366	\end{lemma}
367	
368	\begin{proof}
369	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1755-L1759).
370	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (common denominator).}
371	  The polynomial \(p\) has finitely many non-zero coefficients
372	  \(c_1, \dots, c_r \in K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\). Each \(c_j\) is a fraction
373	  \(c_j = a_j / s_j\) with \(a_j \in A\) and \(s_j \in A \setminus \{0\}\). Put
374	  \(g := s_1 \cdots s_r \in A\); since \(A\) is a domain and each \(s_j \ne 0\), also
375	  \(g \ne 0\). For each \(j\) the denominator \(s_j\) divides \(g\), so
376	  \(c_j = a_j / s_j\) lies in the subring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\). Hence every
377	  coefficient of \(p\) lies in \(A_g\), and \(p\) is the coefficient-extension of a
378	  polynomial in \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\).
379	\end{proof}
